questions.question: Prove that the sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral is 360°.
questions.answer: Divide the quadrilateral into two triangles. Sum of angles in each triangle is 180°, so total sum is 180° + 180° = 360°.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of exterior angles of a quadrilateral is 360°.
questions.answer: Each exterior angle forms a linear pair with interior angle. Sum of all exterior angles = 360°.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rectangle are equal.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by diagonals and sides to show diagonals are equal.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a square are equal and bisect each other at right angles.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles and Pythagoras theorem to show diagonals equal and perpendicular.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at right angles.
questions.answer: Draw diagonals, use congruence of triangles to show diagonals intersect at 90°.

questions.question: Prove that opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence in the parallelogram to show opposite sides are equal.

questions.question: Prove that opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal.
questions.answer: Use alternate interior angles formed by a transversal through parallel sides to show equality of opposite angles.

questions.question: Prove that diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other.
questions.answer: Use congruent triangles formed by the diagonals to show they bisect each other.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rectangle bisect each other and are equal.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence to show diagonals are equal and bisect each other.

questions.question: Prove that diagonals of a square bisect each other at right angles.
questions.answer: Use congruence of right triangles formed by diagonals to show they are perpendicular and bisect each other.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rhombus bisect the angles.
questions.answer: Use congruent triangles formed by diagonals to show each diagonal divides angles into two equal parts.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is 180°.
questions.answer: Use the property that exterior angle equals opposite interior angle or inscribed angle theorem to show sum is 180°.

questions.question: Prove that a parallelogram is a rectangle if one angle is 90°.
questions.answer: Use parallel line and transversal property to show all angles are 90°, making it a rectangle.

questions.question: Prove that a rhombus is a square if one angle is 90°.
questions.answer: Since all sides are equal and one angle is 90°, diagonals will also be equal making it a square.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if its diagonals bisect each other.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence formed by diagonals to show opposite sides are equal, hence a parallelogram.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a kite are perpendicular.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by kite sides to show diagonals intersect at right angles.

questions.question: Prove that one diagonal of a kite bisects the other.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles on either side of the main diagonal to show it bisects the other.

questions.question: Prove that the opposite angles between unequal sides of a kite are equal.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence formed by the diagonals to show equality of these angles.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of the squares of the sides of a parallelogram equals the sum of the squares of the diagonals.
questions.answer: Use vector method or coordinate geometry to express sides and diagonals, then apply Pythagoras theorem to show equality.

questions.question: Prove that the line joining the midpoints of opposite sides of a quadrilateral forms a parallelogram.
questions.answer: Use coordinate method or vector method to show midpoints connected form opposite sides parallel.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rhomboid bisect each other but are not perpendicular.
questions.answer: Use triangle congruence to show bisection; show angle between diagonals is not 90°.

questions.question: Prove that a parallelogram circumscribed about a circle is a rhombus.
questions.answer: Use properties of tangent lengths from vertices to the circle to show all sides are equal.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is cyclic if and only if sum of opposite angles is 180°.
questions.answer: Use inscribed angle theorem to show both directions: cyclic implies sum 180°, sum 180° implies cyclic.

questions.question: Prove that in a trapezium, the line joining the midpoints of non-parallel sides is parallel to the bases.
questions.answer: Use coordinate geometry to show that the midline connecting midpoints has slope equal to base slope.

questions.question: Prove that the area of a rhombus is half the product of its diagonals.
questions.answer: Divide rhombus into four right-angled triangles using diagonals, sum their areas to get 1/2 × d₁ × d₂.
